plan.failureSummary: The test failed because the page URL contained a hash fragment (#playwright-check-suite) that was not expected by the assertion. The AI successfully navigated to the correct page, but the search result link it selected was a deep link.
observedIndicators: Assertion mismatch on URL hash fragment: expected base URL but received URL with #playwright-check-suite
page.ai objective 'Do a search for \"Playwright check suite\"...' was marked complete successfully
Historical data shows 100% pass rate (17/17) until this run, suggesting a change in search result indexing or AI selection preference
The tool call log shows the AI explicitly clicked a link containing the hash: [href='/docs/constructs/playwright-check#playwright-check-suite']

remediationSteps.summary: Update URL assertion to be more flexible regarding hash fragments.
remediationSteps.details: Change the strict .toEqual() assertion to .toContain() or use a regex to allow for valid hash fragments in the URL. For example: expect(page.url()).toContain('https://www.checklyhq.com/docs/constructs/playwright-check/');

plan.notes: The AI followed the instructions correctly. The search for 'Playwright check suite' returned a result that deep-links to a specific section. The test assertion 'expect(page.url()).toEqual(...)' is too strict as it doesn't account for URL fragments. This is a common issue with documentation search results which often link to specific headers.
